subject: Support for i2c rtc mcp794xx.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md line ~4, grouped with 13 commits in single row

impact.effect_if_absent: MiSTer RTC add-on boards using mcp7941x or m41t81 chips on i2c1 bus would not be detected or functional

notes:
PROVENANCE DOC DISAGREEMENT: This commit is listed in patch-provenance.md as part of a GROUPED row with 12 other commits (aa8afe109..077c2c317), violating the grounding contract's requirement to analyze commits independently. This analysis treats it as a single commit.

FUNCTIONAL ASSESSMENT: This commit adds two RTC devices (&i2c1 rtc_at_68 and rtc_at_6F) using bare compatible strings ('m41t81' and 'mcp7941x', without vendor prefixes). Vanilla 6.18.38 supports these chips via rtc-m41t80.c and rtc-ds1307.c drivers. The bare compatible strings work via a fallback mechanism in of_i2c_register_devices() → of_i2c_get_board_info() → of_alias_from_compatible(), which extracts the device name and matches against i2c_device_id tables: both 'm41t81' and 'mcp7941x' exist in those tables (/mnt/source/linux/drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:m41t80_id and rtc-ds1307.c:ds1307_id). Equivalent functionality exists in vanilla 6.18.38: drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:41 has { 'm41t81', ... } and drivers/rtc/rtc-ds1307.c has { 'mcp7941x', mcp794xx }.

CARRIER PATCH CORRECTION: The carrier patch 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch (/mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch:180-182) supersedes this commit with vendor-prefixed compatible strings ('st,m41t81' and 'microchip,mcp7941x'), which are the proper device tree bindings that match the of_device_id tables in vanilla. The patch provenance document §3.1 line 62 correctly lists commit 2548c2978 as one of the de10-nano DTS follow-ups that were consolidated into patch 0004 during forward-port P1.7.

DISPOSITION RATIONALE: This commit is NOT carried as a separate patch; its functionality is ABSORBED into 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch. The commit's changes are superseded by the carrier patch's corrected compatible strings. Dropping this commit is deliberate—the feature it adds (RTC support) is preserved via the carrier patch but with improved/corrected device tree bindings.

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR IF ABSENT: If this commit were reverted in the fork, the RTCs would not be added to &i2c1. However, since the fork later switched from &i2c1 to i2c-gpio (commit 1337de1fd), this functionality is moot. In our 6.18.38 carry, RTCs are added via the i2c-gpio bus (0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch lines 170-183), which is more robust than the raw &i2c1 approach and uses the correct vendor-prefixed compatible strings.

sonnet-verified: carry-mapping CONFIRMED. Re-read 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch directly: rtc@6f { compatible = "microchip,mcp7941x"; reg = <0x6f>; } is at patch lines 180-182 exactly as cited, and rtc@68 { compatible = "st,m41t81"; ... } at lines 175-178. Cross-checked both vendor-prefixed compatible strings against the live vanilla driver ID tables: drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:103 has of_device_id.compatible="st,m41t81", drivers/rtc/rtc-ds1307.c:1142 has compatible="microchip,mcp7941x". Also confirmed the bare-string fallback claim: drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:77 i2c_device_id has "m41t81", drivers/rtc/rtc-ds1307.c:1079 i2c_device_id has "mcp7941x" -- both match this commit's original (pre-0004) bare compatible strings. Re-read the original fork commit diff (`git show 2548c2978`) and confirmed it matches the record's description exactly (stm,m41t81 -> bare m41t81, plus new rtc_at_6F/mcp7941x node). No corrections needed.
